steps.title: Compare all measures
steps.content: The placebo-aware group has higher mean ratings than the control group on all three measures: 5.0 vs. 3.9, 92.00 vs. 46.00, and 11.4 vs. 5.4. The passage says higher ratings indicate greater well-being.

steps.title: Reject partial readings
steps.content: Choice A lists a range without comparing groups. Choice B says two measures were lower, reversing the data. Choice C gives the control group's lower values and denies greater well-being. The remaining choice captures the all-three-measures pattern. The answer is D.
